issues.title: Stateful Async Client Reused Across Event Loops
issues.symptom: Celery workers or API requests intermittently crash with event loop mismatch errors.
issues.root_cause: A stateful async AI provider client was cached globally and got reused across different event loops.
issues.resolution: Removed unsafe global caching and reinitialized clients per valid loop context.
issues.lesson_learned: Do not globally cache loop-bound async clients in mixed API + worker systems.

issues.title: Beat Scheduler Started on Multiple Worker Replicas
issues.symptom: Periodic cleanup and maintenance tasks execute multiple times in a cluster.
issues.root_cause: Celery Beat was enabled on more than one worker replica.
issues.resolution: Limited Beat startup to a single designated worker instance.
issues.lesson_learned: Distributed schedulers must run as a singleton.

issues.title: Redis-Dependent Objects Created at Import Time
issues.symptom: API startup crashes or route import failures when Redis is not ready.
issues.root_cause: Redis-backed objects such as rate limiters were constructed at module import time.
issues.resolution: Moved construction to dependency injection / runtime factory paths.
issues.lesson_learned: Avoid side-effectful infrastructure initialization at import time.

issues.title: Reserved SQLAlchemy Attribute Name
issues.symptom: Application startup fails with SQLAlchemy InvalidRequestError related to `metadata`.
issues.root_cause: An ORM model used `metadata` as a mapped Python attribute name.
issues.resolution: Renamed the Python attribute to `artifact_metadata` while preserving DB column mapping where needed.
issues.lesson_learned: Never map reserved SQLAlchemy names directly as ORM attributes.

issues.title: Qdrant Document ID Drift
issues.symptom: Upload appears successful but retrieval/delete by document ID fails or returns zero matches.
issues.root_cause: LlamaIndex auto-generated ref_doc_id/doc_id values that no longer matched PostgreSQL document IDs.
issues.resolution: Pinned LlamaIndex source document IDs explicitly to the PostgreSQL `document_id`.
issues.lesson_learned: Cross-system joins require explicit stable IDs.

issues.title: Double Chunking in Ingestion
issues.symptom: Sections are fragmented into overly small chunks and retrieval quality drops.
issues.root_cause: Markdown parse logic and the ingestion pipeline both applied chunk splitting.
issues.resolution: Kept chunking in one place only: the ingestion pipeline.

issues.title: Worker Startup Broke Due to Noisy Hardware Detection Output
issues.symptom: Celery worker fails because concurrency/autoscale values are parsed as invalid strings.
issues.root_cause: Third-party hardware libraries printed extra text to stdout during shell command substitution.
issues.resolution: Captured only the final line and validated numeric values before using them.
issues.lesson_learned: Always sanitize subprocess stdout before using it as config.

issues.title: Allowed Origins Textarea Lost Newlines
issues.symptom: Platform admin cannot press Enter to add multiple origins line by line.
issues.root_cause: Frontend normalized `allowed_origins` on every keystroke, immediately collapsing blank newline state.
issues.resolution: Switched to draft textarea state and only parse lines on load/save.
issues.lesson_learned: Keep raw multiline form state until submit time.

issues.title: Frontend Crash on Admin Dashboard Health Card
issues.symptom: The `/admin` page renders a blank error screen with `Object.entries(undefined)`.
issues.resolution: Frontend now supports both shapes and uses safe fallbacks when data is missing.
issues.lesson_learned: Health/status contracts should tolerate additive backend shape changes.

issues.title: Root Docs Drifted Away From Actual Project State
issues.symptom: README, AGENTS, and architecture docs describe single-tenant, TEI-only, session-based behavior that no longer exists.
issues.root_cause: Large refactor landed faster than top-level documentation updates.
issues.resolution: Rewrote root docs to match the current multi-tenant, stateless, OpenAI-compatible architecture.
issues.lesson_learned: Large refactors need documentation closure before release tagging.

issues.title: Redis Restart Loop From Corrupted AOF Tail
issues.symptom: Redis container keeps restarting and logs `Bad file format reading the append only file appendonly.aof.1.incr.aof`.
issues.root_cause: The incremental AOF tail inside the Redis volume became corrupted, so Redis could not finish loading append-only persistence.
issues.resolution: Stopped Redis, backed up the volume, repaired the AOF with `redis-check-aof --fix`, then restarted the stack.
issues.lesson_learned: When Redis runs with AOF persistence in Docker, abrupt shutdowns can corrupt the AOF tail. Back up first, then repair the AOF instead of blaming application code.

issues.title: Multi-Query Retrieval Caused 4x Embed + Rerank Overhead with Negligible Benefit
issues.symptom: Chat turns cost ~4s of pure retrieval overhead. LLM responses showed no measurable accuracy gain from the extra retrieved context, while the public benchmark surfaced no citation-quality improvement either.
issues.root_cause: `IngestionService._build_retrieval_queries` built up to `retrieval_history_query_count + 1` queries (1 latest + 1 combined-concatenated + up to 3 previous user messages) and submitted each through the full embed+rerank pipeline. The `combined` query was literal newline-joined user text, not a semantic rewrite, so the extra query added noise. The LLM already receives the full recent message history via `ai_max_history_messages` in the system prompt, so multi-query RAG duplication was effectively redundant. The code had no documented design rationale — git history showed it was generated by an AI coding assistant (ChatGPT) and landed alongside unrelated features (LlamaParse, HyDE, query expansion, AI usage tracking) without isolation.
issues.resolution: Reduced `_build_retrieval_queries` to return only the latest user query by default. Lowered `RETRIEVAL_HISTORY_QUERY_COUNT` default to `0` and relaxed its validator to `0..5` (0 = no history aggregation, latest-only). The setting is preserved as an opt-in escape hatch for cases where multi-query is empirically justified. LLM conversation context (separate from retrieval) remains governed by `ai_max_history_messages=6` and is untouched.
issues.lesson_learned: AI-generated code that introduces multi-step RAG patterns (query expansion, multi-query, HyDE) must be empirically benchmarked before being kept as the default. When the LLM already has full conversation history, history-aggregated retrieval is usually redundant. Treat non-obvious AI-suggested defaults as opt-in, not baseline.

issues.title: Flat Vector-Only Retrieval Missed Numbered ERP Sections
issues.symptom: Queries like `4.5`, `4.6.3`, hoặc tên mục tính năng dễ rơi vào section sai hoặc thiếu child section quan trọng.
issues.root_cause: Pipeline cũ coi manual như semantic text phẳng: một collection, payload tối giản, không có section graph chuẩn, không có route section -> chunk theo cấu trúc.
issues.resolution: Rebuilt RAG around canonical `document_sections`, dual Qdrant collections (`documents_sections`, `documents_chunks`), structure-aware metadata, sentence-window chunk retrieval, recursive section expansion, và auto-merging về parent section.
issues.lesson_learned: Tài liệu ERP/manual không nên được index như text phẳng. Nếu muốn trả lời đúng heading/section, phải mang cấu trúc tài liệu vào ingestion và retrieval ngay từ đầu.

issues.title: Ingestion Recursion Triggered by Oversized Parser Metadata
issues.symptom: Document upload reached worker ingestion but failed with `maximum recursion depth exceeded` or metadata-length warnings during chunk parsing.
issues.root_cause: Deep section metadata such as `breadcrumb` and `breadcrumb_text` was passed directly into LlamaIndex parsers, consuming too much of the effective chunk budget for ERP manuals.
issues.resolution: Split parser metadata from retrieval metadata. Chunk parsers now receive only a minimal metadata subset, and full retrieval metadata is attached after node generation. A guarded fallback keeps sentence-window chunking alive if hierarchical support hits recursion depth.
issues.lesson_learned: Parser metadata and retrieval metadata serve different purposes; keeping parser metadata intentionally small makes ingestion much more stable.

issues.title: Cached Async Qdrant Client Reused Across Celery Worker Event Loops
issues.symptom: Worker ingestion fails with "Event loop is closed" / "ResponseHandlingException: Event loop is closed" when calling Qdrant. The first task succeeds but subsequent tasks crash.
issues.root_cause: `get_async_qdrant_client()`, `get_section_vector_store()`, and `get_chunk_vector_store()` in `app/core/llama_index.py` were decorated with `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)`. Celery workers use `asyncio.run()` which creates a fresh event loop per task. The cached `AsyncQdrantClient` held a reference to the old (closed) event loop's internal `httpx.AsyncClient`, causing the crash on the next task.
issues.resolution: Removed `@lru_cache` from `get_async_qdrant_client()`, `get_section_vector_store()`, `get_chunk_vector_store()`, and `get_vector_store()`. The sync `get_qdrant_client()` kept its cache since it has no event loop dependency.
issues.lesson_learned: Never cache async clients that internally manage HTTP sessions (httpx.AsyncClient, aiohttp.ClientSession, etc.) in mixed sync+async environments where event loops are created per-task (Celery + asyncio.run()). Each asyncio.run() invocation must get a fresh async client.
